goal: Add a side-effect-free Bluepilot intake surface that validates and normalizes Maya/Kaya autonomy authority decisions before handing them to activation-decision operator mode.
scope_out: Do not call Maya/Kaya live, call providers, execute runtime, write files/GitHub/database, persist receipts, issue permits, merge, deploy, change packages, change workflows, or reopen retired sandbox write routes.
claims: Bluepilot can validate and normalize a Maya/Kaya autonomy authority decision without becoming the source of truth.
A valid decision must carry allowed status, authority references, subject, autonomy mode, grant scope, ethics charter reference, safety evidence reference, and the shared hard-stop policy.
Expired, missing, mismatched, or incomplete decisions fail closed before activation-decision handoff.
The new route exposes only contract and preflight surfaces and does not call Maya/Kaya live.
assumptions: Maya-core will implement the canonical decision issuer/verifier in a separate repo task.
Activation-decision operator mode remains the local Bluepilot executor decision layer after intake normalization.
dod: Contract and preflight route for Maya/Kaya autonomy authority intake are exposed.
Valid Maya/Kaya full-access evidence produces a normalized activation-decision handoff.
Missing, expired, mismatched, or hard-stop-policy-incomplete evidence blocks.
Focused tests, typecheck, task-lock verification, diff-check, and full builder tests pass.
stop_conditions: Live Maya/Kaya call, provider call, runtime execution, file/GitHub/database write, durable persistence, permit issue, merge, deploy, package change, workflow change, or sandbox write reopening becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: Bluepilot now consumes Maya/Kaya autonomy decisions in activation-decision operator mode. It still needs a dedicated intake/normalization surface before the activation-decision handoff.
